title: angelica arguta var. breweri
intro: Angelica arguta var. breweri, also known as Brewer's angelica, is a perennial herb in the carrot family, native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating a variety of health issues. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving menstrual cramps, and easing respiratory problems. Brewer's angelica can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in various ways.
